behaviors.description: SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to the correct sum of the referenced range
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is inside the SUM function's range-aggregation logic — a single evaluator function, not a module boundary. REAL-INFRA: No OS, network, or subprocess involved. LLM-DEP: No AI/ML. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence points to Unit; output is Unit. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: the behavior is self-contained in the formula evaluator; no cross-module handoff is required to surface this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an in-memory cell grid, assign known numeric values to A1:A10, parse and evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)`, assert the result equals the expected arithmetic sum.

behaviors.description: Cascaded cross-cell formula references (e.g., =B1+1 where B1=A1*2) produce the correct result
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure only appears when the formula evaluator, cell reference resolver, and cell store hand off data across boundaries — B1's formula must be evaluated and its result stored before C1's formula can consume it. Mocking the cell store hides this interaction bug. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network needed; an in-process wired cell store is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network → not System. (b) No AI/ML → not Agentic. (c) No multi-step agentic chain → not Workflow. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Integration; output = Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire together the formula parser, evaluator, and cell store; set A1=5, set B1=`=A1*2`, set C1=`=B1+1`; evaluate C1 and assert the result is 11.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1→B1→A1) is detected and returns a circular reference error instead of looping
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Cycle detection is a pure graph algorithm (DFS/topological-sort). The failure — infinite loop or missing error — lives in that algorithm, not at a module handoff boundary. The dependency graph can be constructed directly without parsing real cell formulas. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network. LLM-DEP: No. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: the behavior text targets detection correctness ('detects a circular dependency and returns a circular reference error') — the graph algorithm is the sole failure site. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Unit; output = Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a dependency graph directly with A1 depending on B1 and B1 depending on A1; invoke the cycle-detection routine; assert a circular-reference error is returned and no infinite loop occurs.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with exact match returns the correct value; returns an error when the key is absent
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Both the hit and miss paths are internal to the VLOOKUP function's search logic — a single function over an in-memory table. No cross-module handoff is required to surface either failure. REAL-INFRA: None. LLM-DEP: None. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: VLOOKUP operates on a cell range value that can be provided directly without the full cell store wiring. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Unit; output = Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Build an in-memory lookup table; invoke VLOOKUP with a key that exists and assert the correct column value is returned; invoke again with a missing key and assert the appropriate error value (e.g., #N/A) is returned.

behaviors.description: Nested IF formula evaluates the correct branch for all condition ranges
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Nested IF is a parsing and AST-evaluation problem; all branches live inside the formula evaluator. The full range of outcomes is exercisable by varying the input cell value, with no cross-module handoff involved. REAL-INFRA: None. LLM-DEP: None. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: the behavior is purely about the evaluator's conditional branching logic. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Unit; output = Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to values representing each branch (e.g., 5, 15, 25); evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` for each; assert the result matches the expected branch string.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE skips empty cells rather than inserting blank strings
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The skip-empty behavior is internal to the CONCATENATE function's handling of null/empty cell values — a single function's conditional logic. No module boundary crossing is needed to expose this bug. REAL-INFRA: None. LLM-DEP: None. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: CONCATENATE receives a list of cell values; that list can be supplied directly without the full cell store. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Unit; output = Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Pass an argument list containing non-empty strings interspersed with empty/null values to CONCATENATE; assert the result contains only the non-empty values joined without blank gaps.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (Sheet2!A1) resolves correctly
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The `Sheet2!A1` syntax requires the formula evaluator to cross a module boundary into the workbook/sheet manager to locate Sheet2 and retrieve A1. The failure — wrong value or unresolved reference — only appears when these two modules are wired together. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network; an in-process multi-sheet workbook object is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network. (b) No AI/ML. (c) No multi-step agentic chain. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Integration; output = Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2 wired through the real sheet manager; set Sheet2!A1=10; evaluate `=Sheet2!A1+1` in Sheet1; assert the result is 11. Also assert an appropriate error is returned when the referenced sheet does not exist.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas recalculate in the correct topological order
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Correct topological ordering requires the dependency graph, the recalculation scheduler, and the cell evaluator to coordinate. Mocking any one of these hides ordering bugs — the failure only manifests when they are integrated. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network required; the full dependency pipeline can run in-process. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network. (b) No AI/ML. (c) No multi-step agentic chain. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Integration; output = Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain A1→B1 (=A1*2)→C1 (=B1+1); mutate A1 to a new value; assert B1 and C1 reflect correctly updated values and that intermediate values were consistent at each recalculation step.

behaviors.description: Undo after a formula edit restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Restoring 'all previously computed dependent values' requires the undo/redo stack, cell store, formula text storage, and dependency recalculator to work together. The failure — dependent values not restored, or formula text and computed values diverging — only appears when all these modules are integrated. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network needed. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network. (b) No AI/ML. (c) No multi-step agentic chain. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Integration; output = Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Establish cells with formulas and dependents; record pre-edit values; edit a formula; invoke undo; assert that both the formula text of the edited cell and the computed values of all dependent cells are identical to the pre-edit state.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas with no floating-point precision loss
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The serializer, binary encoder, and deserializer must all be exercised end-to-end for data-loss and precision bugs to surface. REAL-INFRA: The failure mode is in the custom binary format encoding/decoding logic, not in OS filesystem behavior. An in-process BytesIO buffer reproduces precision and data-loss bugs without real disk I/O. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) Real disk I/O is not required to reproduce the stated failure — BytesIO suffices for the encoding/precision bug; a temp file could optionally be used but is not the failure boundary. (b) No AI/ML. (c) No agentic chain. COMMIT-TO-REASONING: Evidence-level = Integration; output = Integration. System probability is non-trivial because real file I/O edge cases (buffering, partial writes) exist, but are not the stated failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a workbook with cells containing integers, floats, strings, and formulas; serialize to a BytesIO buffer using the real binary-format encoder; deserialize using the real decoder; assert every cell's value and formula string matches the original, and that floating-point values are bit-for-bit identical.
